dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond for a cardiac arrest or death at --Address Redacted-- in Richmond.
Hi, I'm Medic Alex with the rescue squad. What's the reason you called 911 today?
Chief complaint is altered mental status with no response to painful stimulus.
Unresponsive to pain as per GCS 3, eye 1, verbal 1, motor 1.
Airway compromised by tongue - sonorous respirations evident.
Respiration rate at 4, shallow and non-labored. Signs of airway obstruction.
SpO₂ at 30%, pulse 100, weak regular carotid. Skin cyanotic and clammy.
Glucose is absent on initial assessment.
Respirations improved to 12 post-intervention.
SpO₂ improved to 97% after BVM and O2.
EtCO₂ at 60mmHg initially. Airway improved after head-tilt-chin-lift.
Initiate BVM assisted ventilations with high-flow oxygen mask.
Giving BVM with O₂ and positioning airway at sniffing angle.
Initiating 4-lead ECG monitoring now.
12-lead EKG obtained - inverted leads and atrial tachycardia.
Skin is pale, cyanotic, and clammy. Warm to the touch.
Pupils are pinpoint at 1-2mm bilaterally. Altered mental status noted.
IV access started in left antecubital with an 18G.
Giving 0.1mg Naloxone IV for opioid overdose.
Pupils at 2-3mm, now reactive to light and GCS improved to 6.
BP was 0/0 initially and improved to 151/85 later.
Mental status now E2 (eye opening to pain), V1 (no verbal response), M4.
No allergies recorded. History of opioid misuse reported by a bystander.
Applying physical restraints using four cravats for safety during transport.
Glucose recorded as 125 mg/dL after naloxone.
Administered second dose of 0.1mg Naloxone IV while enroute to hospital.
Skin color normalized, now warm and dry.
SpO₂ maintained at 98% on 4 liters via nasal cannula.
EtCO₂ decreased to 50mmHg with sustained improvement.
12-lead shows sinus tachycardia after naloxone administration.
Radial pulse now present and strong. SpO₂ stable at 98%.
Patient is secured with five-point restraints. Ready for transport.
Will transport to Chippenham Hospital Cold.
Vent fan is active for airborne precautions. PPE includes respirator, eye protection, and gloves.
No pain reported. Pain score at 0. No vocal complaints.
SpO₂ drops to 0% suddenly. Reverting to BVM support.
BP at 115/65, pulse steady at 88 after naloxone.
SpO₂ recovered to 97% with 4LPM. EKG back to sinus rhythm.
PPE and ambulance precautions complete. Travel to emergency department is planned.
Final assessment completed. All procedures followed.
Transporting to closest ED for further care.
Again, no pain reported. The patient has not vocalized discomfort.
Attempted airway support using 28Fr nasopharyngeal insertion; met resistance.
26Fr nasopharyngeal device reinserted but again met resistance during attempt.
Placed in recumbent position to support airway during transport.
Transport initiated with continued BVM support by RFD.
Two more minutes enroute, vital signs to be checked once more before arrival.
Monitoring occurs with sustained sinus rhythm and normal labored breathing.
No additional deterioration noted during transport.
Arrival imminent at Chippenham Hospital Cold.
Five-point restraint confirmed secure. No change in status.
Vent fan remains on to maintain airborne precautions during transport.
PPE components ensured and no risk of contamination during patient transfer.
EMS protocols reaffirmed and patient care report transmitted to receive team.
Carefully log all findings for hospital handoff during transfer.
Confirming all vitals accurate and consistent with documented timeframes.
Reminding team of ongoing need to monitor for complications.
Once at ED, medical team to evaluate for signs of respiratory distress or further instability.
Confirming all time-based vitals and events were documented per protocol.
All resources and equipment have been returned to ambulance post-patient offload.
Ongoing communication with RFD and ER team ensured.
Final sign-off completed with receiving hospital.
Preventative signatures with patient and family submitted.
Remaining protocol decisions documented for next care team.
ECRM update notes ready to be dispatched after receiving facility sign-off.
Submission of care summary packet complete.
Primary patient afterward was well managed and stable at transfer.
Transport events tracked from 16:28 to 17:07.
Patient status updated in EPiCS for follow through in ED.
Paramedics and EMTs to meet for shift change and post-incident briefing.
Assisted patient up inside RFD truck before securing into stretcher.
Stretcher rest on floor secured before covering guard rail assemblies.
Patient moved using RFD apparatus to EMS and ambulance for transport operations.
Completing all logbook parameters assigned with patient call begin and end time.
Emergency services officially concluded at 17:07, patient stable in care.
Final documentation of case number, primary care team, and notes.
Case now closed in driving system with patient moved to facility care.
All incident data compiled to non-public safety server for hospital access.
Prepped ambulance for future response. Equipment restocked and charged.
Receiving response calls as per normal procedures.
Confidential counseling with patient and family to be discussed post ED visit.
All information and care process submitted for hospital readmission review if needed.
Patient cared for in full with appropriate and timely interventions.
EMS care complete, no further action or follow-up is expected.
This concludes the care provided by EMS for the patient.
All patient data has been properly archived and shared with appropriate departments.
We are now available for the next emergency call in our assigned coverage area.
Data collection and clinical tasks completed on patient file per log entry norms.
All EMS responder safety protocols followed per incident, including PPE and restraints.
No breach of active oath or standard enacted during care delivery phase.
Patient relationship and communication protocols followed as successful.
This report and patient care will be reviewed for quality control as ultimately required.
EMS services terminated at 17:07 and incident officially closed.
